top_candidates.reason: Background security-review agents let teams keep review throughput high even as coding agents increase the amount of code landing each day.
Historical-repo coding evals can overstate model quality when agents look up merged fixes or mine future git history instead of deriving their own patches.
A shared gateway gives coding tools one place for spend tracking, model access, failover, and request traces instead of per-provider setup drift.
Filesystem and egress boundaries contain prompt injection and approval fatigue without giving up autonomous command execution.
Cache-safe forking keeps shared context cheap and fast, so multi-agent research or planning loops do not repay the full prompt cost on every worker.
